summary: Threat intelligence report for IOC 142.11.206.73. Severity: LOW. Type: IOC Reputation / Correlation Lookup. 1 suspicious indicators identified. 1 threat intelligence correlation(s) identified.

mentions_found: [THREAT INTEL] Target "142.11.206.73" correlates with SILKBELL (Axios Supply Chain) campaign (CRITICAL, March 30, 2026)

correlation.description: SILKBELL (Axios Supply Chain) campaign targeting Cross-platform credential theft, crypto wallets

mitre_attack.description: Data exfiltrated via same HTTP/HTTPS/Socket channel used for C2 communication
mitre_attack.reference: SILKBELL: Exfiltrates via HTTP POST to sfrclak.com:8000. API tokens, credentials, wallet data sent in POST body

detection.yara:
rule SUSP_NodeJS_Malware_Unknown : malware_nodejs_rat
{
    meta:
        description = "Automatically generated YARA rule for Node.js malware detection"
        author = "Security Talent Threat Intel v2.0"
        date = "2026-05-05"
        reference = "Security Talent Threat Intel"
        c2_ips = "142.11.206.73"
        hash_md5 = ""
        hash_sha256 = ""
    
    strings:
        $s1 = "142.11.206.73" wide ascii
    
    condition:
        any of them
}
detection.sigma:
title: Node.js Malware Detection - IOC IP Address
id: sigma-mosio1ke
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects indicators of Node.js malware based on analysis of sample.
    Generated by Security Talent Threat Intel v2.0.
    Correlated with known campaigns (NodeCordRAT, SILKBELL, ClickFix, LofyGang).
author: Security Talent Threat Intel (Automated)
date: 2026-05-05
references:
    - Security Talent Threat Intel
    - https://www.zscaler.com/es/blogs/security-research/malicious-npm-packages-deliver-nodecordrat
    - https://socket.dev/blog/axios-npm-package-compromised
    - https://socprime.com/active-threats/clickfix-to-maas-inside-a-modular-windows-rat-and-its-control-panel/
    - https://research.jfrog.com/post/lofygang-returns-a-dual-payload-npm-package/
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_node:
        Image|endswith: '\node.exe'
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'execSync'
            - 'child_process'
            - 'http'
            - '.onion'
            - 'grpc'
            - 'socks5'
            - 'DownloadString'
            - 'WebClient'
    selection_network:
        - Image|endswith: '\node.exe'
        - DestinationIp|contains:
            - '142.11.206.73'
    selection_registry:
        TargetObject|contains:
            - 'CurrentVersion\\Run\\Node'
            - 'CurrentVersion\\Run\\LogicOptimizer'
    selection_script:
        - Image|endswith: '\cscript.exe'
          CommandLine|contains: '.vbs'
        - Image|endswith: '\wscript.exe'
          CommandLine|contains: '.js'
        - Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
          CommandLine|contains:
            - 'node'
            - 'npm'
            - 'axios'
    condition: 1 of selection_*
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate Node.js development activity
    - Authorized npm package installations
level: high

recommendations: Block all identified C2 domains and IPs at network perimeter firewall and DNS sinkhole.
Review firewall and proxy logs for any historical connections to identified C2 infrastructure.
Run full EDR/AV scan on all systems that may have been exposed to this sample.
Review all npm dependencies for typosquatted or suspicious package names.
Implement npm package verification: use `npm audit`, `socket.dev`, or `npm vet` in CI/CD pipelines.
Enable runtime application self-protection (RASP) for Node.js applications.
Apply the generated YARA and Sigma rules for proactive threat hunting.
Rotate any credentials, API keys, tokens, or secrets that may have been exposed.
